generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) serving as the primary mechanical actuator in the MABI MAX 100 first axis, providing torque and angular velocity inputs to the gear transmission system."
]

instance GearWheelMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearwheel component in the transmission system that transmits torque from the motor to the cycloidal gearbox, enabling speed reduction and torque multiplication."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component that further reduces speed and increases torque output for the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot, incorporating backlash compensation mechanisms."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor input current (I_M) for torque estimation and friction analysis in the motor-gearbox system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance PrimaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft providing real-time angular position data (q_M) for motion tracking and backlash analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PrimaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the cycloidal gearbox providing real-time output angle data (q_out) for positional accuracy verification and backlash compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance Sinumerik840DController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller aggregating and processing time-series data (position, speed, acceleration) from the robot axis for digital shadow integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance PrimaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PrimaryEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance ControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Manufacturer-provided dynamics model for permanent magnet synchronous motor torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) derivation from measured current (I_M) and encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearWheelDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mechanical model describing torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) transmission characteristics between motor and cycloidal gearbox interface."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GearWheelSimulationEnabler
]

instance CycloidalGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox transmission model incorporating backlash and friction characteristics for output torque (τ_out) and angular velocity (ω_out) calculation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GearboxSimulationEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model for motor-gearbox system, accounting for static and kinetic friction components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model capturing stiction and breakaway behavior in the gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model for dynamic friction characterization in the cycloidal gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model providing detailed micro-slip dynamics for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance BaseFrictionlessModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Idealized Simulink model simulating frictionless behavior of the gearbox system for comparative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationComparisonEnabler
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor input current (I_M) data transmitted from the current sensor to the digital shadow for torque estimation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorSimulationEnabler
]

instance PrimaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time angular position data (q_M) from the primary encoder mounted on the motor shaft, used for motion tracking and backlash analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PrimaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorSimulationEnabler
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time output angle data (q_out) from the secondary encoder behind the cycloidal gearbox, used for positional accuracy verification."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GearboxSimulationEnabler
]

instance ControllerPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series position data (q_M, q_G, q_out) from the SINUMERIK 840D controller, including derived quantities for motion analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControllerDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationComparisonEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated motor torque (τ̃_M) and angular velocity (ω̃_M) data generated by the motor simulation models for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorSimulationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearWheelTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearwheel torque (τ̃_G) and angular velocity (ω̃_G) data generated by the gearwheel simulation models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearWheelSimulationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearboxOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated cycloidal gearbox output torque (τ̃_out) and angular velocity (ω̃_out) data for comparative analysis with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxSimulationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationComparisonEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MqttDataStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation enabling real-time data streaming from SINUMERIK 840D controller to the digital shadow for continuous monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionIdentificationService,
                   DataAggregationService,
                   VisualizationService
]

instance SimulinkProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink-based processing platform implementing executable simulation models for friction analysis and dynamics calculation."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorSimulationService,
                   GearWheelSimulationService,
                   GearboxSimulationService,
                   FrictionAnalysisService,
                   SimulationComparisonService
]

instance MotorSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink model processing motor dynamics (τ_M, ω_M) from measured current and encoder data for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorSimulationService
]

instance GearWheelSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink model processing gearwheel dynamics (τ_G, ω_G) from motor output data for transmission analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables GearWheelSimulationService
]

instance GearboxSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink model processing cycloidal gearbox dynamics (τ_out, ω_out) from gearwheel input data for output analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables GearboxSimulationService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink-based friction analysis platform implementing multiple friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) for dynamic characterization."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance SimulationComparisonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink-based comparison platform enabling quantitative analysis of deviations between simulated and measured data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationComparisonService
]

instance UnifiedDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Visualization dashboard aggregating and presenting data traces (measured vs. simulated) for semantic understanding of friction-induced inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous, multi-physics-aware detection of joint friction (static, kinetic, viscous) in the gearbox and motor using white-box and black-box models."
    DTDFVocab:provides StaticFrictionInsight,
                   KineticFrictionInsight,
                   ViscousFrictionInsight,
                   FrictionDeviationAction
]

instance MotorSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executable simulation service processing motor dynamics (τ_M, ω_M) from measured inputs for friction analysis and control parameter updates."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotorDynamicsInsight,
                   FrictionCompensatedModelAction
]

instance GearWheelSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executable simulation service modeling gearwheel dynamics (τ_G, ω_G) for transmission analysis and backlash compensation."
    DTDFVocab:provides GearWheelDynamicsInsight
]

instance GearboxSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executable simulation service modeling cycloidal gearbox dynamics (τ_out, ω_out) for output accuracy analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides GearboxDynamicsInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Friction characterization service implementing multiple friction models to quantify motion inaccuracies (up to 30%) in the drive train."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight,
                   AnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance SimulationComparisonService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service comparing simulated vs. measured data traces to quantify deviations caused by friction, enabling control accuracy improvements."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAccuracyInsight,
                   DeviationAnalysisAction
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service computing higher-level abstractions (τ_G, ω_G) from raw sensor data using executable simulation models."
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueVelocityAbstractionInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating data traces into a unified dashboard for semantic understanding of physical relationships (e.g., friction loss in drive train)."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance TraceabilitySupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service linking data points to their sources (encoders, simulations) and computational models for transparency in decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides TraceabilityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StaticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static friction components in the gearbox and motor, enabling quantitative assessment of motion inaccuracies."
]

instance KineticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of kinetic friction components affecting the drive train dynamics during motion."
]

instance ViscousFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of viscous friction effects in the cycloidal gearbox and motor system."
]

instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of combined friction effects (static, kinetic, viscous) in the gearbox and motor, enabling up to 30% improvement in motion accuracy."
]

instance MotorDynamicsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed analysis of motor dynamics (τ_M, ω_M) derived from measured current and encoder data for friction compensation."
]

instance GearWheelDynamicsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of gearwheel dynamics (τ_G, ω_G) including transmission losses and backlash characteristics."
]

instance GearboxDynamicsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of cycloidal gearbox output dynamics (τ_out, ω_out) including friction and backlash effects."
]

instance ControlAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of control accuracy improvements enabled by friction-compensated dynamics models."
]

instance TorqueVelocityAbstractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Higher-level abstractions of torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) computed from raw sensor data for system analysis."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic understanding of physical relationships through unified visualization of measured vs. simulated data traces."
]

instance TraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Transparency in decision-making through complete data lineage and source attribution for all processed information."
]

instance FrictionCompensatedModelAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Indirectly informs the SINUMERIK 840D sl controller with friction-compensated dynamics models for model-based control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionDeviationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Provides friction-compensated dynamics models to the CNC controller for real-time motion correction."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Detects anomalies (e.g., sudden friction increases due to wear) via deviation analysis between simulated and measured traces."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeviationAnalysisAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Quantifies deviations between simulated and measured data traces to identify friction-induced inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, encompassing mechanical components and control systems within [-90°, 90°] working range."
    base:contains MotorActuator,
               GearWheelMechanism,
               CycloidalGearbox,
               MotorCurrentSensor,
               PrimaryEncoder,
               SecondaryEncoder,
               Sinumerik840DController
]

// C13: Components
instance MabiMax100FirstAxis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot comprising permanent magnet synchronous motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox for high-precision manufacturing tasks."
]

instance FrictionAnalysisSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized digital shadow system implementing multiple friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) for real-time friction characterization."
]

instance SimulationComparisonSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis system enabling quantitative assessment of deviations between simulated and measured data traces for control accuracy improvements."
]

// C14: Data Transmitted (Additional)
instance MotorSimulationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearWheelSimulationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearWheelSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearboxSimulationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxSimulationEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on **real-time and near-real-time scales**:
  - **Data acquisition**: Sensor data (e.g., encoder readings, current) is streamed at **high-frequency intervals** (exact rate not specified but implied to be synchronized with the robot’s control loop, likely **<100ms**).
  - **Model computation**: Simulation models (e.g., friction calculations) are executed **dynamically** during operation to update friction parameters in response to **operational changes** (e.g., wear, tool changes).
  - **Visualization updates**: Dashboard refreshes are **time-synchronized** with the physical system’s operation to enable **real-time analysis** of friction-induced inaccuracies.
  - **Synchronization**: The digital shadow’s **data traces** are aligned with the physical system’s **operational timeline**, ensuring **temporal coherence** between simulated and measured behaviors."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is organized as a **purpose-driven, centralized digital shadow** with the following orchestration:
  - **Core Components**:
    1. **FrictionShadow (Identification Element)**: Acts as the **container** for the digital shadow’s purpose (**FrictionDerivation**).
    2. **Asset (Mabi)**: Represents the **physical first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, described via **SysML SystemModel**.
    3. **Data Sources**:
       - **Physical Sources**: Encoders, current sensor, CNC controller (via MQTT).
       - **Virtual Sources**: Simulation models (SimModel_M, SimModel_GW, SimModel_GB).
    4. **Processing Components**: Simulink-based **Simulation** classes that process input data traces (e.g., **I_M**) and generate output traces (e.g., **τ̂_M**) using **friction models**.
  - **Data Flow**:
    - **Unidirectional (PT → DS)**: Data flows from the physical system (sensors, CNC) to the digital shadow via **MQTT**.
    - **Internal Processing**: Simulation models **consume input traces** (e.g., **I_M**) and **produce output traces** (e.g., **τ̂_M**) with **traceable lineage** (Fig. 5).
  - **Visualization**: A **dashboard** integrates all data traces (measured and simulated) for **real-time analysis** of friction-induced deviations."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **as-operated phase** of the physical twin’s lifecycle, focusing on **real-time monitoring and analysis during active manufacturing tasks**. It does not model:
  - **As-designed (ideal) phase**: No virtual prototype or CAD-based digital twin is constructed.
  - **As-manufactured phase**: While the digital shadow incorporates **manufacturer-provided parameters** (e.g., motor dynamics), it does not explicitly model deviations introduced during manufacturing (e.g., gearbox tolerances).
  The digital twin is **operationally aligned** with the physical system’s runtime, enabling **continuous friction model refinement** throughout the robot’s operational lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**:
    - **Simulated vs. Measured Data Comparison**: Deviations between **measured (r_M, r_G, r_out)** and **simulated (τ̂_M, τ̂_G, τ̂_out)** traces quantify **friction-induced inaccuracies**.
    - **Friction Model Accuracy**: White-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models are **continuously refined** during operation to adapt to **wear, load, or temperature changes**.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**:
    - **Sensor Noise**: Encoder and current sensor inaccuracies may affect **τ_M** and **ω_M** calculations.
    - **Model Parameters**: Manufacturer-provided dynamics models may include **simplifications** (e.g., ignored backlash effects).
    - **Backlash**: Mitigated via **dual encoder arrangement** (motor + secondary encoder) but not fully modeled in the digital shadow.
  - **Verification Mechanisms**:
    - **Traceability**: Each **data trace** is linked to its **source** (e.g., encoder channel) and **processing model** (e.g., SimModel_M) via **metadata**.
    - **Dashboard Visualization**: Enables **real-time validation** of simulated vs. measured behavior.
  - **Error Handling**: The digital shadow does not explicitly describe **error correction mechanisms**, but **model refinement** during operation implicitly addresses **fidelity gaps**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) is established via:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for **real-time data streaming** from the **SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller** to the digital shadow.
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data (e.g., **I_M**, output angle) is transmitted as **structured payloads** with **time-stamps**.
  - **Synchronization**: Data is **time-aligned** with the physical system’s operation to ensure **coherent analysis** of friction-induced deviations.
  - **Architecture**: **Client-Server Model**:
    - **Client**: Physical system (robot + CNC controller) acts as the **data provider**.
    - **Server**: Digital shadow (Simulink-based processing) acts as the **data consumer**.
  - **Latency**: Implicitly low due to **real-time control loop synchronization**, though exact latency values are not specified."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is **hosted locally** on a **computational platform** (likely the same as the Simulink environment) connected to the **SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller** via **MQTT**. Deployment specifics include:
  - **Hardware**: Not explicitly described, but the **Simulink models** are executed on a **real-time capable system** (e.g., embedded PC or industrial PC) to ensure **low-latency processing**.
  - **Software Stack**:
    - **MATLAB/Simulink**: Runs the **executable simulation models** and **data processing components**.
    - **MQTT Broker**: Facilitates **real-time data exchange** between the CNC controller and digital shadow.
    - **Dashboard**: Implemented as a **visualization layer** (tools not specified but likely **MATLAB App Designer** or **third-party dashboards**).
  - **Scalability**: The centralized architecture is **not explicitly designed for scalability**, but the **modular Simulink models** could theoretically support **additional axes** if extended."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Used as a **concrete realization** (Fig. 5) to define **Purpose, DataTrace, Source, Asset, Processing, and Model** concepts (Michael et al., 2020).
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implicitly referenced for structuring the digital shadow’s components, though not explicitly decentralized in this use case.
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Standard for **structural and behavioral modeling** of the first axis (Fig. 3).
  - **MQTT Protocol**: Industry-standard for **real-time data streaming** in industrial IoT applications.
  - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)**: Follows **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** methodology for **multi-abstraction modeling** (e.g., functional vs. physical layers)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin does not explicitly address **security or safety considerations**, but the following implicit aspects are noted:
  - **No Remote Actuation**: The digital shadow operates as a **passive, one-way data flow system** (per Kritzinger et al., 2018), with **no direct control over the physical system**.
  - **Data Integrity**: **MQTT’s built-in authentication/encryption** (if configured) could protect data in transit, though this is not described.
  - **Safety**: The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller** enforces **safety constraints** (e.g., working range [-90°, 90°], max speed 75°/s), but the digital twin does **not intervene** in safety-critical operations.
  - **Ethical Risks**: No discussion of **data ownership, privacy, or cybersecurity risks** (e.g., unauthorized access to sensor data)."
]
